claimText: The International Maritime Organization (IMO) is a specialized agency of the United Nations responsible for shipping safety and preventing marine pollution.
evidenceExcerpt: The International Maritime Organization (IMO) is a UN specialized agency responsible for shipping safety and preventing marine pollution.
examUse: Statement-based question on UN specialized agencies and their mandates.
distractorHooks: IMO is a subsidiary of the WTO
IMO focuses exclusively on maritime trade tariffs

claimText: India is a member state of the International Maritime Organization (IMO).
examUse: Statement-based question regarding India's membership in international organizations.
distractorHooks: India is only an observer state
India is not a member of the IMO

claimText: The Maritime Safety Committee (MSC) is the highest technical body of the International Maritime Organization (IMO).
evidenceExcerpt: Its highest technical body is the Maritime Safety Committee (MSC).
examUse: Statement-based question on the structure and bodies of the IMO.
distractorHooks: MSC is a body under the International Seabed Authority
MSC is the highest decision-making body of the UNCLOS

claimText: The Hong Kong Convention (2009) is an IMO convention that regulates the safe and environmentally sound recycling of ships.
evidenceExcerpt: Key conventions include SOLAS (1974), MARPOL, and the Hong Kong Convention (2009) for safe and environmentally sound ship recycling.
examUse: Correct-pair question matching international conventions to their specific environmental or maritime purposes.
distractorHooks: Hong Kong Convention regulates deep sea mining
Hong Kong Convention deals with marine plastic pollution
Hong Kong Convention is related to maritime piracy
